Class AbstractFilterExpressionConverter

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.filter.converter.AbstractFilterExpressionConverter
All Implemented Interfaces:
FilterExpressionConverter
Direct Known Subclasses:
AzureAiSearchFilterExpressionConverter, CouchbaseAiSearchFilterExpressionConverter, ElasticsearchAiSearchFilterExpressionConverter, GemFireAiSearchFilterExpressionConverter, MariaDBFilterExpressionConverter, MilvusFilterExpressionConverter, MongoDBAtlasFilterExpressionConverter, Neo4jVectorFilterExpressionConverter, OpenSearchAiSearchFilterExpressionConverter, PgVectorFilterExpressionConverter, PineconeFilterExpressionConverter, PrintFilterExpressionConverter, RedisFilterExpressionConverter, SqlJsonPathFilterExpressionConverter, TypesenseFilterExpressionConverter, WeaviateFilterExpressionConverter
public abstract class AbstractFilterExpressionConverter extends Object implements FilterExpressionConverter
AbstractFilterExpressionConverter is an abstract class that implements the FilterExpressionConverter interface. It provides default implementations for converting a Filter.Expression into a string representation. All specific filter expression converters should extend this abstract class and implement the remaining abstract methods. Note: The class cannot be directly instantiated as it is abstract.
Author:
Christian Tzolov

    ISO_DATE_FORMATTER
    Formatter for parsing and normalizing ISO date strings.
    protected static final Pattern
    ISO_DATE_PATTERN
    Pattern for ISO-8601 date strings in UTC (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z') used to recognize and normalize date strings before passing to converters.

    • doValue

      Convert the given value into a string representation.

      filterValue - the value to convert
      context - the context to append the string representation to

    • normalizeDateString

      protected static Object normalizeDateString(Object value)
      If the value is a string matching the ISO date pattern, parse and return as Date so that all converters that handle Date automatically support date strings. Otherwise return the value unchanged.
      Parameters:
      value - the value (possibly a date string)
      Returns:
      the value, or a Date if the value was a parseable date string

    • doSingleValue

      protected abstract void doSingleValue(Object value, StringBuilder context)
      Convert the given single value into a string representation and append it to the context. This method handles all value types including String, Number, Boolean, Date, etc.

      For convenience, implementations can use the provided static helper methods such as emitJsonValue(Object, StringBuilder) for JSON-based filters, emitLuceneString(String, StringBuilder) for Lucene-based filters, or implement their own format-specific escaping logic as needed.

      Parameters:
      value - the value to convert
      context - the context to append the string representation to

    • emitLuceneString

      protected static void emitLuceneString(String value, StringBuilder context)
      Emit a string value formatted for Lucene query syntax by appending escaped characters to the provided context. Used by Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, and GemFire VectorDB query string filters.

      Lucene/Elasticsearch query strings require backslash-escaping of special characters: + - = ! ( ) { } [ ] ^ " ~ * ? : \ / & | < > as well as spaces.

      Parameters:
      value - the string value to format
      context - the context to append the escaped string to
      See Also:
      • Elasticsearch Reserved Characters

    • emitJsonValue

      protected static void emitJsonValue(Object value, StringBuilder context)
      Emit a value formatted as JSON by appending its JSON representation to the provided context. Used for PostgreSQL JSONPath, Neo4j Cypher, Weaviate GraphQL, and other JSON-based filter expressions.

      This method uses Jackson's ObjectMapper to properly serialize all value types:

      • Strings: properly quoted and escaped with double quotes, backslashes, and control characters handled
      • Numbers: formatted without quotes (e.g., 42, 3.14)
      • Booleans: formatted as JSON literals true or false
      • null: formatted as JSON literal null
      • Other types: handled according to Jackson's default serialization
      Parameters:
      value - the value to format (can be any type)
      context - the context to append the JSON representation to

    • doGroup

      protected void doGroup(Filter.Group group, StringBuilder context)
      Convert the given group into a string representation.
      Parameters:
      group - the group to convert
      context - the context to append the string representation to

    • hasOuterQuotes

      protected boolean hasOuterQuotes(String str)
      Check if the given string has outer quotes.
      Parameters:

      Returns:
      true if the string has outer quotes, false otherwise

    • removeOuterQuotes

      protected String removeOuterQuotes(String in)

      Parameters:
      in - the string to remove the outer quotes from
      Returns:
